title: sesuvium maritimum
intro: Sesuvium maritimum, also known as the Port Jackson or Sea Purslane, is a succulent plant native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its small, pink to purple flowers and its ability to thrive in coastal and salt-affected areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Sesuvium maritimum include reducing inflammation, improving skin conditions, and supporting immune function. Sesuvium maritimum is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical creams and ointments.
